title: Using hardcoded IP addresses is security-sensitive
description: Using hardcoded IP addresses is security-sensitiveAnalyze your coderesponsibility-trustworthysecuritySecurity HotspotHardcoding IP addresses is security-sensitive. It has led in the past to the following vulnerabilities:CVE-2006-5901CVE-2005-3725Today’s services have an ever-changing architecture due to their scaling and redundancy needs. It is a mistake to think that a service will always have the same IP address.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Hardcoded IP addresses are security-sensitive. Use configuration or DNS names instead

logic.detection_conditions: String literal containing a hardcoded IPv4 address
logic.triggering_conditions: IPv4 address pattern in string literal (excluding reserved addresses)
logic.reasoning: Hardcoded IP addresses make code inflexible and create security risks. IP addresses can change due to infrastructure changes, and hardcoded IPs may expose internal network topology. Use configuration, environment variables, or DNS names instead. Reserved addresses like localhost and broadcast are excluded as they have fixed meanings.
